steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best emphasizes when she collected both samples. The answer must highlight the timing of both collections, not location, significance, or any other detail.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "While both specimens collected by Ynés Mexía are members of the same family, *Erigeron maximus* was found in Minas Gerais and *Brickellia diffusa* was found in Jalisco.": omits both collection dates and focuses on family/location. ✗
  • "Ynés Mexía collected a sample of *Erigeron maximus* in February of 1930, after collecting *Brickellia diffusa* in November of 1926.": gives both collection dates and links them with 'after' to show their chronological relationship. ✓
  • "On February 1 in 1930, Ynés Mexía added a new specimen to her growing collection of botanical samples: *Erigeron maximus* of the Asteraceae family.": gives timing for only Erigeron maximus and omits when Brickellia diffusa was collected. ✗
  • "Thousands of botanical samples collected by Ynés Mexía can now be found in one place: online at the C.V. Starr Virtual Herbarium.": describes where the samples are now, not when they were collected. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
